plans.descEn: One juz per day for a month.
About 104 verses a day — half a juz per day, paced for steady reading without overwhelm.
About 17 verses a day. The most sustainable pace — a quiet daily habit you can keep alongside everything else.
The 30th juz, the most-memorised section of the Quran. One short surah (or two) per day. Approachable for memorisation.
About 38 entries a day from Imam Ali's sermons, letters, and sayings. Two-month pace through one of the cornerstones of Shia literature.
About 13 hadith a day from Shaykh al-Mufid's recorded teaching assemblies. 387 hadith total — a compact entry point to early Shia hadith literature.
Imam al-Sajjad's Treatise on Rights, one section per day. A short, reflective week on the rights and responsibilities of a believer.
Volume 1 of Al-Kafi — the foundations: intellect, knowledge, oneness, prophecy, the Imamate. About 16 hadith a day for a quarter of the year.
Shaykh al-Saduq's major work on Divine Oneness — about 19 hadith a day across the foundational theology of the Imami school.
Al-Saduq's traditions organised numerically — 'the one', 'the two', up through hundreds and thousands. About 22 hadith a day for two months.
Al-Husayn al-Ahwazi's compilation on asceticism and detachment. About 10 short hadith a day for a contemplative month.
Shaykh al-Saduq's recorded teaching assemblies — 1,082 hadith at about 18 a day. The companion volume to al-Mufid's Amali.
Al-Husayn al-Ahwazi's brief compilation on the believer — the rights, virtues, and trials of one who has faith. About 14 hadith a day for two weeks.
Shaykh al-Tusi's massive jurisprudential collection — about 36 hadith a day across a full year. One of the Four Books, completed by daily discipline.

plans.titleEn: Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih in a Year
plans.descEn: Shaykh al-Saduq's foundational jurisprudential collection — 'For Him Who Has No Jurist Present.' About 17 hadith a day across a full year. One of the Four Books.
Shaykh al-Tusi's resolution of apparently-contradicting hadiths. About 23 hadith a day for half a year. The fourth of the Four Books — completes the set alongside Tahdhib, al-Faqih, and al-Kafi.
Al-Saduq's 'Springs of the Reports of al-Rida' — narrations from and about the 8th Imam. About 16 hadith a day for two months.
